methodology: ISCO-08 task descriptions scored by Claude Haiku 4.5. Employment from Eurostat lfsa_egai2d 2023. Wages from Eurostat earn_ses_annual 2022. Outlook from Cedefop European Skills and Jobs Survey (directional estimates).

occupations.name: Chief executives, senior officials and legislators
occupations.rationale: Strategic decision-making and budget oversight are highly cognitive and digitizable, but leadership requires significant in-person stakeholder engagement, real-time human judgment in complex situations, and organizational presence that remains difficult for AI to fully substitute.

occupations.name: Administrative and commercial managers
occupations.rationale: HR managers perform primarily cognitive work (recruitment planning, budget management, policy compliance) that is largely digitizable and automatable, though employee relations, negotiations, and strategic consulting require some human judgment and interpersonal presence that provide partial barriers to full automation.

occupations.name: Production and specialised services managers
occupations.rationale: Primarily cognitive work (budgeting, monitoring, contracting, sales coordination) that could be partially automated, but requires significant on-site physical presence for inspections, facility management, and real-time operational oversight of aquaculture/fisheries systems.

occupations.name: Hospitality, retail and other services managers
occupations.rationale: Retail and wholesale trade managers require significant in-person presence for staff supervision, floor operations, and customer relationship management, but face high AI exposure in inventory management, purchasing decisions, compliance monitoring, and sales analytics.

occupations.name: Science and engineering professionals
occupations.rationale: Primarily cognitive work (research, analysis, writing) that can be digitized, but requires physical lab work, field specimen gathering, and direct observation of living organisms, which creates meaningful barriers to full automation.

occupations.name: Health professionals
occupations.rationale: Specialist medical practitioners require significant in-person patient interaction, physical examination, and real-time clinical judgment that AI cannot fully replace, though diagnostic and administrative tasks face moderate automation pressure.

occupations.name: Teaching professionals
occupations.rationale: Vocational teachers require significant in-person presence for hands-on instruction, demonstrations, and real-time monitoring of student performance in technical skills, though lesson planning, assessment preparation, and some theoretical instruction are increasingly automatable.

occupations.name: Business and administration professionals
occupations.rationale: Policy Administration Professionals perform primarily cognitive work (analysis, research, proposal writing, risk assessment) that is largely digital and automatable, but retain moderate human elements through stakeholder consultation, judgment calls on feasibility, and the need for contextual understanding of political and organizational dynamics that currently require human expertise.

occupations.name: ICT professionals
occupations.rationale: Software developers perform primarily digital work with automatable components (code generation, testing, documentation), though creative problem-solving, architectural decisions, and client collaboration provide some resistance to full automation.

occupations.name: Legal, social and cultural professionals
occupations.rationale: Librarians perform primarily cognitive work (cataloguing, research, information management) that is increasingly automatable through AI systems, but retain significant human elements in user service, collection curation decisions, and community engagement that require physical presence and interpersonal judgment.

occupations.name: Science and engineering associate professionals
occupations.rationale: Primarily cognitive work with significant digital components (analysis, design, monitoring), but requires on-site physical presence for specimen collection, equipment operation, and real-time safety oversight in mining environments.

occupations.name: Health associate professionals
occupations.rationale: Nursing associates require physical presence for hands-on patient care (administering medications, wound cleaning, monitoring), but substantial portions like documentation and care planning are increasingly automatable, creating a moderate AI exposure profile.

occupations.name: Business and administration associate professionals
occupations.rationale: Trade brokers perform primarily cognitive, digital work (market analysis, communications, negotiations) that can be done remotely, but retain some exposure mitigation through relationship-building, real-time judgment calls in volatile markets, and the need for specialized domain expertise and trust that's harder to automate.

occupations.name: Legal, social, cultural and related associate professionals
occupations.rationale: Social work requires significant in-person client interaction, real-time human judgment, and emotional intelligence that AI cannot replicate, though documentation, resource coordination, and some assessment tasks are digitizable.

occupations.name: Information and communications technicians
occupations.rationale: Primarily cognitive digital work (troubleshooting, documentation, remote support) with high automation potential, though real-time human judgment and occasional physical hardware interaction provide moderate barriers.

occupations.name: General and keyboard clerks
occupations.rationale: This occupation involves entirely digital work products (typing, transcription, document formatting) that are highly automatable through speech-to-text, OCR, and AI writing assistance tools, with minimal requirement for human judgment or physical presence.

occupations.name: Customer services clerks
occupations.rationale: Receptionists perform primarily digital and cognitive tasks (scheduling, data entry, information lookup) that are highly automatable through AI chatbots and automated systems, though their role requires some in-person presence and interpersonal judgment that provides moderate buffering.

occupations.name: Numerical and material recording clerks
occupations.rationale: Transport clerks perform primarily digital record-keeping, coordination, and reporting tasks that are largely automatable through logistics management systems and AI, though some real-time operational coordination and human judgment in complex scenarios provide modest barriers to full substitution.

occupations.name: Other clerical support workers
occupations.rationale: Scribes perform primarily digital transcription and form completion work that is entirely computer-based and highly automatable by OCR, speech-to-text, and language models, with minimal human judgment required.

occupations.name: Personal service workers
occupations.rationale: Transport conductors require physical presence on vehicles, real-time human judgment in emergencies, and direct passenger interaction, but ticket collection and basic administrative tasks face moderate AI automation risk.

occupations.name: Sales workers
occupations.rationale: Shopkeepers blend cognitive tasks (ordering, budgeting, pricing) that are automatable with essential physical presence requirements (stock management, customer interaction, real-time decision-making in physical space) that create meaningful AI exposure barriers.

occupations.name: Personal care workers
occupations.rationale: Requires physical presence, hands-on manual care (hygiene, positioning, lifting), and real-time human judgment despite some administrative tasks that could be automated.

occupations.name: Protective services workers
occupations.rationale: Security guards require physical presence, real-time situational awareness, and in-person authority to patrol premises, identify threats, and respond to incidents—tasks that cannot be performed remotely and demand human judgment in dynamic environments.

occupations.name: Market-oriented skilled agricultural workers
occupations.rationale: Crop growing requires substantial physical presence for soil preparation, planting, tending, and harvesting, with weather-dependent timing and hands-on judgment that cannot be automated or done remotely, though marketing and administrative tasks have moderate AI exposure.

occupations.name: Market-oriented skilled forestry
occupations.rationale: Deep-sea fishery work requires constant physical presence on vessels, skilled manual labor in hazardous conditions, real-time decision-making based on weather and ocean conditions, and hands-on fish handling that cannot be automated or performed remotely.

occupations.name: Subsistence farmers, fishers, hunters and gatherers
occupations.rationale: Subsistence livestock farming requires constant physical presence, hands-on animal handling, real-time judgment in unpredictable environments, and manual skills that cannot be automated or performed remotely.

occupations.name: Building workers
occupations.rationale: Floor layers and tile setters require physical presence on-site, manual dexterity, spatial judgment, and skilled hand-tool operation that cannot be automated or performed remotely, making AI exposure minimal.

occupations.name: Metal, machinery workers
occupations.rationale: This occupation requires significant physical presence on-site, manual skill with heavy machinery and materials, and real-time spatial judgment that cannot be performed remotely or by AI, making it highly resistant to automation despite some potential for AI-assisted planning tools.

occupations.name: Handicraft and printing workers
occupations.rationale: Potter and jewelry work requires significant hands-on craftsmanship, physical presence, and manual dexterity that cannot be automated, though some design and technical aspects increasingly use digital tools.

occupations.name: Electrical and electronic trades workers
occupations.rationale: Electrical mechanics require on-site physical presence, hands-on manual skill with tools and equipment, and real-time problem-solving in complex installations, which are substantial barriers to AI automation despite some potential for diagnostic support.

occupations.name: Food processing, wood working
occupations.rationale: This occupation requires physical presence, sensory evaluation (taste and smell), and real-time human judgment that cannot be replicated digitally, making it largely resistant to AI automation despite some potential for measurement and documentation tasks.

occupations.name: Stationary plant and machine operators
occupations.rationale: Plant operators require physical presence on-site for equipment monitoring, manual operation of machinery, threading materials, and real-time troubleshooting of complex industrial systems that cannot be fully automated or remotely controlled, creating a natural barrier to AI substitution.

occupations.name: Assemblers
occupations.rationale: While some tasks like data recording are digitizable, the core work requires physical presence, manual dexterity, hand-eye coordination, and real-time sensory inspection that resist automation despite advances in robotics.

occupations.name: Drivers and mobile plant operators
occupations.rationale: Bus and tram drivers require constant physical presence, manual operation of vehicles, real-time navigation and safety decisions in dynamic environments, and direct passenger interaction—all substantial barriers to AI automation despite some autonomous vehicle development.

occupations.name: Cleaners and helpers
occupations.rationale: Vehicle cleaning requires physical presence, manual dexterity, and hands-on work that cannot be performed remotely or by current automation technology.

occupations.name: Agricultural, forestry and fishery labourers
occupations.rationale: Livestock farm labour requires constant physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time decision-making based on animal behavior and health assessment, creating a strong natural barrier to AI automation.

occupations.name: Labourers in mining, construction, manufacturing and transport
occupations.rationale: Civil engineering labourers perform primarily manual, physical work on construction sites that requires on-site presence, specialized physical skills, and real-time spatial judgment that cannot be automated or performed remotely.

occupations.name: Food preparation assistants
occupations.rationale: Kitchen helpers require significant physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time coordination in food preparation and cleaning tasks that cannot be performed remotely or automated without substantial infrastructure investment.

occupations.name: Street workers
occupations.rationale: Street vending requires physical presence, manual handling of goods, real-time customer interaction, and location-based service delivery that cannot be automated or performed remotely, creating strong natural barriers to AI substitution.

occupations.name: Refuse workers and other elementary workers
occupations.rationale: Refuse sorting requires physical presence, manual dexterity, real-time sensory judgment to identify materials, and direct handling of waste materials—tasks that cannot be performed remotely or by AI without advanced robotics.
